summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 7 warning, 9 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `bin/lib/runbooks.mjs:12` — Custom YAML parser (67 lines) will need rewriting if schema evolves beyond flat structure. Document this constraint.

🟡 `bin/lib/runbooks.mjs:196` — `_filename` leaks internal sort metadata into the public runbook data shape. Future consumers may accidentally depend on it. Consider encapsulating tie-break logic or documenting it as internal.
🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:418` — `planTasks()` has no direct unit tests. Task shape mapping, `runbookFlow` return, and fallthrough behavior verified only by code reading.
🟡 SPEC.md:22 — AC says `matchRunbook(description, runbooksDir)` but implementation correctly takes `runbooks` (array) per Technical Approach line 72. Update AC param name to match actual API.

🟡 `bin/lib/runbooks.mjs`:110 — `isSafeRegex` heuristic misses alternation-based ReDoS patterns like `(a|a)+$` (verified: 4,255ms on 25 chars). Low exploitability — requires repo write access to plant malicious YAML, at which point the attacker can already run arbitrary code.

🔴 `bin/lib/runbooks.mjs:258` — **Premature abstraction**: `selectRunbook` has exactly 1 production caller. It wraps `matchRunbook` with a 3-line forced-id branch that belongs inline in `planTasks()`. Inline it and import `matchRunbook` directly.
🟡 `bin/lib/runbooks.mjs:194` — `flow` field is plumbed through 5 code points but zero shipped runbooks use it.
🟡 `bin/lib/runbooks.mjs:273` — `include` task field is implemented/tested but unused by any shipped runbook.
